leads.title: WEATHER IMPACT ALERT: Chance for rain will increase Thursday night into Friday and continuing through Saturday causing possible flooding
leads.summary: A cold front is positioning itself over South Carolina and it will impact our weather through Saturday with a chance for a lot of rain, possibly leading to flooding

leads.title: Charleston weather: Thunderstorms and flash flooding possible Friday across the Charleston area
leads.summary: CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) – Storm Team 2 has declared a Weather Alert Day for Friday and Saturday afternoons due to the potential for thunderstorms and flash flooding. A slow-moving cold front is forecast to interact with the sea breeze on Friday afternoon, which could produce heavy rainfall across portions of the Lowcountry. Forecasters say rainfall [...]

leads.title: Some parts of South Carolina may experience flash flooding this weekend
leads.summary: After some severe weather and significant rainfall on July 21, you can expect it to continue on July 24 into the weekend, leading to potential flooding in parts of South Carolina.

leads.title: Flash Flood Warning issued July 24 at 1:29AM EDT until July 24 at 4:30AM EDT by NWS Columbia SC
leads.summary:
FFWCAE

The National Weather Service in Columbia has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
West Central Lancaster County in central South Carolina...

* Until 430 AM EDT.

* At 129 AM EDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 1.5 and 2.5 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 1 to 2 inches in 1
hour. Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are possible in
the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Fla

leads.title: FIRST ALERT WEATHER DAY: Flood Watch in effect from noon to 9:00 PM
leads.summary: Scattered downpours, along with a few stronger storms, will populate across the Lowcountry beginning Friday!

leads.title: North Charleston Fire Department, Police investigating fatal house fire
leads.summary: Crews with the North Charleston Fire Department responded to a house fire on Thursday night.

leads.title: Three people dead after house fire in North Charleston Thursday night: NCFD
leads.summary: NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) - Three people are dead after a house fire in North Charleston on Thursday night, according to Christian Rainey, Deputy Chief of the North Charleston Fire Department (NCFD). Around 9:06 p.m., multiple agencies responded to a home in the 5200 block of Sterrett St. that was showing smoke and flames. Deputy [...]

leads.title: Crashes slow traffic in area of Highway 31, Highway 501
leads.summary: HORRY COUNTY, S.C. (WBTW) -- No serious injuries were reported after multiple crashes that are slowing traffic in the area of Highway 31 and Highway 501, Horry County Fire Rescue said. Traffic on Highway 31 near Highway 501, back to Carolina Forest Boulevard, is backed up, HCFR said. There are several crashes in the area, [...]

leads.title: Crews respond to fire at Florence Chevrolet dealership
leads.summary: FLORENCE, S.C. (WBTW) -- Florence Fire Department responded Thursday morning to a commercial structure fire at 2199 David H. McLeod Boulevard, the address of Five Star Chevrolet Florence. According to the Fire Marshal, firefighters arrived at about 6:30 a.m. to find smoke coming from the side of the building. Crews entered the building to begin [...]

leads.title: Firefighters contain 40% of Horse Island wildfire
leads.summary: BERKELEY COUNTY, S.C. (WCBD) – Authorities say firefighters have contained 40% of the 1,011-acre Horse Island Fire on the Francis Marion National Forest near Shulerville. On July 23, 2026, firefighters will continue mopping up hot spots near containment lines. Authorities say many trees are burning out at the roots and falling over, presenting a hazard to [...]

leads.title: NCPD arrests two men after finding machine gun conversion device, cocaine in hotel room
leads.summary: NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) - The North Charleston Police Department (NCPD) arrested two men on Monday after finding multiple firearms, including a machine gun conversion device, and illegal drugs during an investigation at a local motel. On July 20, just before 1:30 p.m., officers responded to the Siesta Motel on Rivers Avenue after receiving a [...]

leads.summary: A firefighter was hurt after an early morning fire.

leads.title: Loris-area car crash sets nearby woods on fire, officials say
leads.summary: HORRY COUNTY, S.C. (WBTW) -- A Loris-area vehicle crash caused a woods fire late Wednesday night, according to Horry County Fire Rescue. Crews responded just before 9 p.m. to the area of Riverbend Road and Highway 141 where a vehicle involved in a crash caught fire, which set nearby woods on fire. The incident was [...]

leads.title: Flood Watch Continues as Showers, Storms Bring Cooler Temperatures to the Carolinas
leads.summary: (WSPA) -- Mostly cloudy and not as hot Thursday. Showers and thunderstorms are expected, with the highest concentration over the mountains and the northern Upstate. This will lead to a spread of high temperatures, with mid-to-upper 70s in the mountains and a range from mid-80s in the northern Upstate to lower 90s in the south. [...]

leads.title: Waccamaw Boulevard bar owner gives more detail about weekend shootout
leads.summary: HORRY COUNTY, S.C. (WBTW) -- The owner of a bar along Waccamaw Boulevard in Horry County has provided new details about a shootout over the weekend that left a person wounded. Officers were called to the 3200 block of Waccamaw Boulevard just after 11:30 p.m. Sunday and found evidence of gunfire, police said. The area [...]

leads.title: Francis Marion Forest wildfire partially contained. Plus, Mount Pleasant police recover stolen cars.
leads.summary: In today’s news briefing, read the latest on the wildfire in the Francis Marion National Forest, Lindsey Graham’s funeral plans and more.

leads.title: Meteor shower famous for fireballs dazzles SC sky
leads.summary: Every clear night offers the chance to see 100 shooting stars per hour, some colored a deep teal-green, and big fireballs so close to Earth, stargazers may be able to hear them sizzle as they tear into Earth’s atmosphere.

leads.title: Smoke from Canadian wildfires triggers air quality concerns in SC. What you should know.
leads.summary: Residents of some South Carolina counties are being cautioned that those sensitive to air quality issues could be affected as smoke from Canadian wildfires rolls into the state.
